1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: the FULL-CORPUS gate for the slice must pass, not a
43	   fixture subset (see "Full-corpus gates" below). A narrow fixture suite that
44	   passes while the corpus gate fails is not acceptance.
45	4. Integration: the open side may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
46	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
47	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
48	   with them (history retains them under GPL). Every integrator that touches a
49	   slice with a full-corpus gate MUST run that gate before declaring green.
50	
51	## Full-corpus gates (standard, not optional)
52	
53	Each slice has a fast oracle gate over the whole 419-file example corpus,
54	diffing the slice's output against the Haskell reference cache. These are the
55	sealed implementer's primary data source (run between build and the HS cache
56	every iteration) AND the integrator's mandatory acceptance check. Both take
57	`RS_PATH` (point at any build) and `ALLOWLIST` (subset while iterating).
58	
59	- Wellformedness: `scripts/wf_gate.sh` — runs the binary WITHOUT `--prove`
60	  (the wf block prints at theory-load, ~1s/file; full corpus ~72s at JOBS=6),
61	  extracts the `/* WARNING … */` block, diffs vs `scripts/.hs_file_cache`.
62	- Web: `scripts/web_parity.sh` — crawls HS and RS interactive servers per
63	  theory, semantic-diffs the response manifests vs `scripts/.web_hs_cache`.
64	- Full batch (proof output, slow ~30-60min): `scripts/corpus_file_diff.sh` —
65	  the ground-truth `--prove` byte diff; run at milestones, not every iteration.
66	
67	Once a slice's ported code is DELETED, the integrated module IS the sealed
68	reimplementation, so running the gate against the tree observes the sealed
69	code's own output vs the reference — legitimate feedback, not observation of
70	replaced code. For a not-yet-integrated slice the tree still holds the ported
71	code, so gate only the workspace build (via `RS_PATH`), never the stock tree.
72

1	//! producers-clean: a clean-room reimplementation of the tamarin-prover
2	//! interactive web UI's fragment PRODUCERS — the code that renders pre-computed
3	//! prover values into the HTML/JSON response-body CONTENT that the already
4	//! clean-roomed dispatch layer serves. Derived purely from black-box
5	//! observation of captured responses + the live oracle. See ../../SPEC.md for
6	//! the observable boundary and the R1–R5 sub-target decomposition, and
7	//! workspace/BEHAVIOR.md for the behavioral spec you build up as you probe.
8	//!
9	//! Status: R1 (center section fragments + the shared HTML skin) IMPLEMENTED —
10	//! `html` (escape / postprocess / envelopes) and `section` (`render_pane`,
11	//! `render_help_pane`), gated by tests/round1_center_fragments.rs (fixtures
12	//! pinned to observed bytes) and tests/corpus_sweep.rs (reassembly byte-parity
13	//! over all 81 capture manifests). R2–R5 remain UNIMPLEMENTED stubs.
14	//!
15	//! Order: R1 (done) → R2 (proof-script west pane) → R3 (proof-tree / method
16	//! HTML) → R4 (welcome / housekeeping) → R5 (theory-path grammar).
17	//!
18	//! In scope: pure-render producers — given pre-computed prover values, emit the
19	//! fragment bytes (tags, links, headings, escaping, line breaks, envelope).
20	//! OUT of scope (opaque input / other clusters): the pretty-printed content
21	//! text itself (formula / rule / signature / method text), the constraint
22	//! system and applicable-proof-methods panes, graph DOT/SVG, and the Rust-only
23	//! progressive-UI route.

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Slice fragment families out of the captured response corpus.
3	
4	The captured corpus (`oracle/captured_responses/*.hs.json`, the sanctioned
5	channel PROTOCOL permits reading) holds, per theory, a crawl manifest:
6	
7	    { base, capped, lemmas, log, manifest: { "<url>": {kind,status,body} } }
8	
9	`<url>` carries the literal token `#` where the server's version index goes;
10	`kind` is one of html | text | json | dot; a `json` body is itself the JSON
11	envelope string `{"html":..,"title":..}` (or `{"redirect":..}`/`{"alert":..}`).
12	
13	This helper lets the sealed room materialize the *exact response bytes* for one
14	fragment family without re-crawling — the byte targets a producer must
15	reproduce. It reads ONLY the captured corpus (no corpus `.spthy`, no source
16	tree); it labels each manifest by the theory name it advertises plus a short
17	content hash so name-collisions (Joux, Scott, …) stay distinct.
18	
19	Usage:
20	  extract_fragments.py list                       # manifests: label / name / kinds
21	  extract_fragments.py families                    # URL families + counts + kinds
22	  extract_fragments.py extract <family[,family2,...]> <outdir> [--only SUBSTR[,SUBSTR2,...]]
23	        # <family> is a URL skeleton, idx-agnostic; pass several comma-separated
24	        # to slice them all in ONE corpus pass, e.g.:
25	        #   overview   main/message   main/rules   main/tactic   main/help
26	        #   main/lemma   source   next   main/cases   main/proof
27	        # writes one file per (manifest, matching-url): <label>__<slug>.<ext>
28	        # json envelopes also emit a sibling .title file (the pane title).
29	  --only SUBSTR  restrict to manifests whose label CONTAINS any listed SUBSTR
30	                 (comma-separated). Omit to take every manifest.
31	
32	All output files are captured program OUTPUT (legitimate byte targets); the
33	`.meta.tsv` index records label -> theory-name -> capture-hash for traceability.
34	"""
35	import json
36	import os
37	import re
38	import sys
39	
40	HERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
41	CORPUS = os.environ.get("CAPTURES", os.path.join(HERE, "captured_responses"))
42	
43	_IDX = re.compile(r"/thy/(trace|equiv)/[^/]+/")
44	
45	
46	def label_of(path, manifest):
47	    """theory-name + 8-char capture hash (collision-proof, capture-only)."""
48	    h = os.path.basename(path).split(".")[0][:8]
49	    name = None
50	    for url, r in manifest.items():
51	        if r.get("kind") == "html" and "/overview/" in url:
52	            m = re.search(r"<title>Theory:\s*([^<]*)</title>", r.get("body", ""))
53	            if m:
54	                name = m.group(1).strip()
55	                break
56	    if name is None:
57	        for url, r in manifest.items():
58	            if r.get("kind") == "text" and url.endswith("/source"):
59	                m = re.match(r"theory\s+(\S+)", r.get("body", ""))
60	                if m:
61	                    name = m.group(1)
62	                    break
63	    name = re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z0-9_.-]", "_", name or "UNKNOWN")
64	    return "%s__%s" % (name, h), name, h
65	
66	
67	def family_of(url):
68	    """idx-agnostic URL skeleton down to the handler section."""
69	    u = _IDX.sub("/thy/trace/#/", url)
70	    m = re.match(r"^/thy/trace/#/(main/[a-zA-Z]+|[a-zA-Z-]+)", u)
71	    if m:
72	        return m.group(1)
73	    return u.strip("/") or "root"
74	
75	
76	def slug(url):
77	    return re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z0-9]+", "_", _IDX.sub("/thy/trace/#/", url)).strip("_")
78	
79	
80	def manifests():
81	    for p in sorted(__import__("glob").glob(os.path.join(CORPUS, "*.hs.json"))):
82	        try:
83	            yield p, json.load(open(p))
84	        except Exception as e:  # noqa
85	            print("skip %s: %s" % (p, e), file=sys.stderr)
86	
87	
88	def cmd_list():
89	    for p, d in manifests():
90	        m = d.get("manifest", {})
91	        lbl, name, h = label_of(p, m)
92	        kinds = sorted({r.get("kind") for r in m.values()})
93	        print("%-40s  %-28s  urls=%-5d  kinds=%s" % (lbl, name, len(m), ",".join(kinds)))
94	
95	
96	def cmd_families():
97	    import collections
98	    fam = collections.Counter()
99	    famk = collections.defaultdict(set)
100	    for _, d in manifests():
101	        for url, r in d.get("manifest", {}).items():
102	            f = family_of(url)
103	            fam[f] += 1
104	            famk[f].add(r.get("kind"))
105	    for f, c in sorted(fam.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
106	        print("%6d  %-24s  %s" % (c, f, ",".join(sorted(famk[f]))))
107	
108	
109	def _write(outdir, base, body, title=None):
110	    os.makedirs(outdir, exist_ok=True)
111	    ext = "html" if ("<" in body or title is not None) else "txt"
112	    with open(os.path.join(outdir, base + "." + ext), "w") as fh:
113	        fh.write(body)
114	    if title is not None:
115	        with open(os.path.join(outdir, base + ".title"), "w") as fh:
116	            fh.write(title)
117	
118	
119	def cmd_extract(family, outdir, only=None):
120	    fams = set(f.strip() for f in family.split(",") if f.strip())
121	    subs = [s.strip() for s in only.split(",")] if only else None
122	    n = 0
123	    meta = []
124	    for p, d in manifests():
125	        m = d.get("manifest", {})
126	        lbl, name, h = label_of(p, m)
127	        if subs and not any(s in lbl for s in subs):
128	            continue
129	        for url, r in m.items():
130	            if family_of(url) not in fams:
131	                continue
132	            body = r.get("body", "")
133	            title = None
134	            if r.get("kind") == "json":
135	                try:
136	                    env = json.loads(body)
137	                except Exception:
138	                    env = None
139	                if isinstance(env, dict) and "html" in env:
140	                    title = env.get("title", "")
141	                    body = env["html"]
142	                # {redirect}/{alert} envelopes are left as raw JSON (no html key).
143	            base = "%s__%s" % (lbl, slug(url))
144	            _write(outdir, base, body, title)
145	            meta.append((base, name, h, url, r.get("kind"), r.get("status")))
146	            n += 1
147	    with open(os.path.join(outdir, ".meta.tsv"), "w") as fh:
148	        fh.write("target\ttheory\thash\turl\tkind\tstatus\n")
149	        for row in meta:
150	            fh.write("\t".join(str(x) for x in row) + "\n")
151	    print("wrote %d '%s' fragments to %s" % (n, family, outdir))
152	
153	
154	def main(argv):
155	    if not argv or argv[0] == "list":
156	        return cmd_list()
157	    if argv[0] == "families":
158	        return cmd_families()
159	    if argv[0] == "extract":
160	        family = argv[1]
161	        outdir = argv[2]
162	        only = None
163	        if "--only" in argv:
164	            only = argv[argv.index("--only") + 1]
165	        return cmd_extract(family, outdir, only)
166	    print(__doc__)
167	    return 2
168	
169

108

110	Exactly two compact shapes, keys in this order, **no** insignificant whitespace,

1	//! Route model for the interactive web UI.
2	//!
3	//! Every observed request path has the shape
4	//! `/thy/<theory-kind>/<index>/<handler>/<args…>` where:
5	//! * `<theory-kind>` is the analysis kind — only `trace` appears in the corpus
6	//!   (a `diff` kind is plausible but unobserved).
7	//! * `<index>` is either `#` (the "current" theory version) or a decimal number.
8	//! * `<handler>` selects the response family. Observed handlers and their
9	//!   response `kind`:
10	//!     - `main/…`                    -> JSON envelope (`{html,title}` / `{redirect}`)
11	//!     - `overview/…`                -> full-page HTML
12	//!     - `intdot/…`                  -> HTML mini-page
13	//!     - `interactive-graph-def/…`   -> DOT
14	//!     - `next/…`, `prev/…`          -> text (a navigation URL)
15	//!     - `autoprove/…`               -> JSON (`{redirect}`), or text on timeout
16	//!     - `source`, `message`         -> text (theory source)
17	//!
18	//! Under `main`, the sub-handlers are: `help`, `message`, `rules`, `tactic`,
19	//! `cases/{raw|refined}/{level}/{n}`, `lemma/{name}`, `add/{pos}`,
20	//! `edit/{name}`, `delete/{name}`, `method/{lemma}/{n}`, and
21	//! `proof/{lemma}/{path…}` (the proof path is a sequence of case-name segments,
22	//! the root being `_`).
23	//!
24	//! This module parses a path into a structured value; it is descriptive (the
25	//! route grammar as observed), not a dispatcher.
26	
27	/// Theory version selector.
28	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
29	pub enum Index {
30	    /// `#` — the server's "current" theory version.
31	    Current,
32	    /// An explicit decimal version index.
33	    Num(u64),
34	}
35	
36	impl Index {
37	    fn parse(s: &str) -> Option<Index> {
38	        if s == "#" {
39	            Some(Index::Current)
40	        } else {
41	            s.parse::<u64>().ok().map(Index::Num)
42	        }
43	    }
44	}
45	
46	/// A `main/*` sub-handler.
47	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
48	pub enum Main {
49	    Help,
50	    Message,
51	    Rules,
52	    Tactic,
53	    Cases { refined: bool, level: usize, n: usize },
54	    Lemma(String),
55	    Add(String),
56	    Edit(String),
57	    Delete(String),
58	    /// Apply proof method `n` at the node identified by `path` (a `/`-joined
59	    /// sequence of case-name segments, root `_`). The method number precedes the
60	    /// path in the URL: `method/{lemma}/{n}[/path…]` (observed live [L8] and in
61	    /// 67988 body links [Q032]).
62	    Method { lemma: String, n: usize, path: Vec<String> },
63	    Proof { lemma: String, path: Vec<String> },
64	    /// Observational-equivalence proof view (`main/diffProof/{lemma}[/path…]`),
65	    /// the diff-mode analogue of `Proof`.
66	    DiffProof { lemma: String, path: Vec<String> },
67	    /// Apply a diff proof method (`main/diffMethod/{lemma}/{n}[/path…]`), the
68	    /// diff-mode analogue of `Method`.
69	    DiffMethod { lemma: String, n: usize, path: Vec<String> },
70	    /// The diff construction/deconstruction rules view (`main/diffrules`).
71	    DiffRules,
72	    /// Any unrecognized `main/*` tail.
73	    Other(Vec<String>),
74	}
75	
76	/// The selected handler and its arguments.
77	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
78	pub enum Handler {
79	    Source,
80	    Message,
81	    Main(Main),
82	    Overview(Vec<String>),
83	    Intdot(Vec<String>),
84	    InteractiveGraphDef(Vec<String>),
85	    Next(Vec<String>),
86	    Prev(Vec<String>),
87	    Autoprove(Vec<String>),
88	    /// Autoprove a single diff lemma (`autoproveDiff/{strategy}/{bound}/diffProof/…`).
89	    AutoproveDiff(Vec<String>),
90	    /// Autoprove all lemmas (`autoproveAll/{strategy}/{bound}`).
91	    AutoproveAll(Vec<String>),
92	    /// Re-read the theory from disk in place (`reload`; POST).
93	    Reload,
94	    /// Download the theory source (`download/{file}`; GET). The filename is a
95	    /// decorative URL segment — the body is always the current theory's source.
96	    Download(String),
97	    /// Append modified lemmas to the on-disk file (`get_and_append/{file}`; POST).
98	    GetAndAppend(String),
99	    /// A structural-edit form target (`edit/{verb}/{name}`; POST). The tail is the
100	    /// `{verb}/{name}` remainder.
101	    Edit(Vec<String>),
102	    /// Delete a theory path (`del/path/{theory-path…}`; GET). The tail is the
103	    /// theory-path segments after the fixed `path` literal. It is parsed into a
104	    /// [`ThyPath`] by the dispatcher (mode-aware): an unparseable tail is a `404`,
105	    /// a parseable one with a non-GET method is a `405`.
106	    DelPath(Vec<String>),
107	    /// Verify a theory path (`verify/{theory-path…}`; GET; trace theories only).
108	    /// The tail is the theory-path segments, parsed into a [`ThyPath`] by the
109	    /// dispatcher.
110	    Verify(Vec<String>),
111	    /// Unrecognized handler with its raw tail.
112	    Other { name: String, tail: Vec<String> },
113	}
114	
115	/// A parsed route.
116	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
117	pub struct Route {
118	    pub theory_kind: String,
119	    pub index: Index,
120	    pub handler: Handler,
121	}
122	
123	fn owned(v: &[&str]) -> Vec<String> {
124	    v.iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect()
125	}
126	
127	fn parse_main(tail: &[&str]) -> Main {
128	    match tail {
129	        ["help"] => Main::Help,
130	        ["message"] => Main::Message,
131	        ["rules"] => Main::Rules,
132	        ["diffrules"] => Main::DiffRules,
133	        ["tactic"] => Main::Tactic,
134	        ["cases", kind, level, n]
135	            if (*kind == "raw" || *kind == "refined")
136	                && level.parse::<usize>().is_ok()
137	                && n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() =>
138	        {
139	            Main::Cases {
140	                refined: *kind == "refined",
141	                level: level.parse().unwrap(),
142	                n: n.parse().unwrap(),
143	            }
144	        }
145	        ["lemma", name] => Main::Lemma((*name).to_string()),
146	        ["add", pos] => Main::Add((*pos).to_string()),
147	        ["edit", name] => Main::Edit((*name).to_string()),
148	        ["delete", name] => Main::Delete((*name).to_string()),
149	        ["method", lemma, n, rest @ ..] if n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() => Main::Method {
150	            lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
151	            n: n.parse().unwrap(),
152	            path: owned(rest),
153	        },
154	        ["diffMethod", lemma, n, rest @ ..] if n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() => Main::DiffMethod {
155	            lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
156	            n: n.parse().unwrap(),
157	            path: owned(rest),
158	        },
159	        ["diffProof", lemma, rest @ ..] => Main::DiffProof {
160	            lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
161	            path: owned(rest),
162	        },
163	        [proof, lemma, rest @ ..] if *proof == "proof" => Main::Proof {
164	            lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
165	            path: owned(rest),
166	        },
167	        _ => Main::Other(owned(tail)),
168	    }
169	}
170	
171	impl Route {
172	    /// Parse a request path such as `/thy/trace/#/main/proof/exec/_/B_2`.
173	    /// Returns `None` if the path is not under `/thy/<kind>/<index>/…`.
174	    pub fn parse(path: &str) -> Option<Route> {
175	        let trimmed = path.strip_prefix('/').unwrap_or(path);
176	        let segs: Vec<&str> = trimmed.split('/').collect();
177	        // Need at least: thy / kind / index / handler
178	        if segs.len() < 4 || segs[0] != "thy" {
179	            return None;
180	        }
181	        let theory_kind = segs[1].to_string();
182	        let index = Index::parse(segs[2])?;
183	        let handler_name = segs[3];
184	        let tail = &segs[4..];
185	        let handler = match handler_name {
186	            "source" => Handler::Source,
187	            "message" => Handler::Message,
188	            "main" => Handler::Main(parse_main(tail)),
189	            "overview" => Handler::Overview(owned(tail)),
190	            "intdot" => Handler::Intdot(owned(tail)),
191	            "interactive-graph-def" => Handler::InteractiveGraphDef(owned(tail)),
192	            "next" => Handler::Next(owned(tail)),
193	            "prev" => Handler::Prev(owned(tail)),
194	            "autoprove" => Handler::Autoprove(owned(tail)),
195	            "autoproveDiff" => Handler::AutoproveDiff(owned(tail)),
196	            "autoproveAll" => Handler::AutoproveAll(owned(tail)),
197	            "reload" => Handler::Reload,
198	            "download" => match tail {
199	                [file] => Handler::Download((*file).to_string()),
200	                _ => Handler::Other { name: "download".to_string(), tail: owned(tail) },
201	            },
202	            "get_and_append" => match tail {
203	                [file] => Handler::GetAndAppend((*file).to_string()),
204	                _ => Handler::Other { name: "get_and_append".to_string(), tail: owned(tail) },
205	            },
206	            "edit" => Handler::Edit(owned(tail)),
207	            // `del/path/<theory-path…>` — the fixed `path` literal must follow.
208	            "del" => match tail {
209	                ["path", rest @ ..] => Handler::DelPath(owned(rest)),

226	/// The full request surface: the top-level (non-`/thy`) routes plus the
227	/// theory-scoped `/thy/<kind>/<index>/…` routes.
228	///
229	/// `Toplevel::parse` takes the URL **path** only (any `?query` must be split off
230	/// by the caller and passed separately to the dispatcher).
231	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
232	pub enum Toplevel {
233	    /// `/` — the index page (GET) / theory upload (POST).
234	    Root,
235	    /// `/robots.txt`.
236	    Robots,
237	    /// `/favicon.ico` — a `303` redirect to the static icon.
238	    Favicon,
239	    /// `/kill` — cancel a running proof search (requires a `path` query arg).
240	    Kill,
241	    /// `/static/<path…>` — a filesystem-backed asset.
242	    Static(Vec<String>),
243	    /// A theory-scoped route.
244	    Thy(Route),
245	    /// Anything else (→ 404).
246	    Other(String),
247	}
248	
249	impl Toplevel {
250	    pub fn parse(path: &str) -> Toplevel {
251	        let trimmed = path.strip_prefix('/').unwrap_or(path);
252	        if trimmed.is_empty() {
253	            return Toplevel::Root;
254	        }
255	        let segs: Vec<&str> = trimmed.split('/').collect();
256	        match segs[0] {
257	            "robots.txt" if segs.len() == 1 => Toplevel::Robots,
258	            "favicon.ico" if segs.len() == 1 => Toplevel::Favicon,
259	            "kill" if segs.len() == 1 => Toplevel::Kill,
260	            "static" => Toplevel::Static(owned(&segs[1..])),
261	            "thy" => match Route::parse(path) {
262	                Some(r) => Toplevel::Thy(r),
263	                None => Toplevel::Other(path.to_string()),
264	            },
265	            _ => Toplevel::Other(path.to_string()),
266	        }
267	    }
268	}

441	/// A theory navigation path, as accepted by the `del/path/…` and `verify/…`
442	/// routes. The accepted heads are **mode-dependent**: a `trace` theory accepts
443	/// `help` · `message` · `rules` · `tactic` · `cases/{raw|refined}/{level}/{n}` ·
444	/// `lemma/{name}` · `proof/{lemma}[/seg…]` · `method/{lemma}/{n}[/seg…]` ·
445	/// `add/{pos}` · `edit/{name}` · `delete/{name}`; an `equiv` (diff) theory accepts
446	/// `help` · `diffrules` · `diffProof/{lemma}[/side…]` · `diffMethod/{lemma}/{n}[/…]`.
447	/// A tail outside the grammar does not parse — the route then answers `404`.
448	///
449	/// Only the lemma and proof nodes carry data the dispatcher needs; every other
450	/// accepted head collapses to [`ThyPath::Other`].
451	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)]
452	pub enum ThyPath {
453	    /// `lemma/{name}` (trace).
454	    Lemma(String),
455	    /// `proof/{lemma}[/seg…]` (trace).
456	    Proof { lemma: String, path: Vec<String> },
457	    /// `diffProof/{lemma}[/side…]` (equiv).
458	    DiffProof { lemma: String, path: Vec<String> },
459	    /// Any other accepted theory path (a navigable view that is neither a lemma
460	    /// nor a proof node).
461	    Other,
462	}
463	
464	impl ThyPath {
465	    /// Parse a theory-path tail. `diff` selects the equiv (diff-theory) grammar.
466	    /// Returns `None` for a tail outside the grammar (the caller then answers a
467	    /// `404`).
468	    pub fn parse(segs: &[String], diff: bool) -> Option<ThyPath> {
469	        let s: Vec<&str> = segs.iter().map(String::as_str).collect();
470	        if diff {
471	            match s.as_slice() {
472	                ["help"] | ["diffrules"] => Some(ThyPath::Other),
473	                ["diffProof", lemma, rest @ ..] => Some(ThyPath::DiffProof {
474	                    lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
475	                    path: owned(rest),
476	                }),
477	                ["diffMethod", _lemma, n, ..] if n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() => Some(ThyPath::Other),
478	                _ => None,
479	            }
480	        } else {
481	            match s.as_slice() {
482	                ["help"] | ["message"] | ["rules"] | ["tactic"] => Some(ThyPath::Other),
483	                ["cases", kind, level, n]
484	                    if (*kind == "raw" || *kind == "refined")
485	                        && level.parse::<usize>().is_ok()
486	                        && n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() =>
487	                {
488	                    Some(ThyPath::Other)
489	                }
490	                ["lemma", name] => Some(ThyPath::Lemma((*name).to_string())),
491	                ["proof", lemma, rest @ ..] => Some(ThyPath::Proof {
492	                    lemma: (*lemma).to_string(),
493	                    path: owned(rest),
494	                }),
495	                ["method", _lemma, n, ..] if n.parse::<usize>().is_ok() => Some(ThyPath::Other),
496	                ["add", _] | ["edit", _] | ["delete", _] => Some(ThyPath::Other),
497	                _ => None,
498	            }
499	        }
500	    }
501	}
502	
503	fn parse_bool(s: &str) -> Option<bool> {
504	    match s {
505	        "True" => Some(true),
506	        "False" => Some(false),
507	        _ => None,
508	    }
509	}

2	# Black-box oracle for the WEB PRODUCER SURFACE clean-room.
3	#
4	# Serves ONE .spthy in the sanctioned Haskell interactive web UI so the sealed
5	# room can curl any route and observe the exact HTTP response body it must
6	# reproduce. Live probing complements the captured corpus (see
7	# ../round1/ + oracle/extract_fragments.py); use it to pin fragment structure,
8	# link targets and section headers a single capture leaves ambiguous.
9	#
10	# Usage:
11	#   hs_server.sh start <file.spthy> [port]   # boot + block until ready; prints URL
12	#   hs_server.sh probe <port> <path>         # curl one route, print the body
13	#   hs_server.sh stop  <port>                # kill the server + free the port
14	#   hs_server.sh smoke <file.spthy> [port]   # start, probe /overview + /main, stop
15	#
16	# Ports 3100-3199 belong to this cluster (default 3100). Everything below is
17	# derived from black-box behavior; no tamarin-rs source is read — the only
18	# tamarin-rs touch is EXECUTING this sanctioned binary.
19	#
20	# Why this wrapper exists (do not "simplify" back): the stock weblayer
21	# hs_server.sh passes `--port <n>` with a SPACE, which the prover's CmdArgs
22	# parser reads as the positional WORKDIR (`directory '<n>' does not exist`) and
23	# the server dies silently after a fixed `sleep 4`. This wrapper (a) uses
24	# `--port=<n>` (equals, like scripts/web_parity.sh), (b) POLLS for readiness
25	# instead of sleeping (maude boot + elaboration routinely take >10s), and
26	# (c) frees the port on stop (pid + process-group + listener fallback).
27	
28	set -u
29	
30	# ---- OOM guards (preserve; a runaway prover must be the kill target) --------
31	echo 1000 > /proc/self/oom_score_adj 2>/dev/null || true
32	ulimit -v 25165824 2>/dev/null || true            # 24 GB address-space cap

36	READY_TIMEOUT="${READY_TIMEOUT:-90}"
37	DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT="${DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT:-30}"    # match web_parity.sh (5s default expires on ~12 files)
38	
39	pidfile() { echo "/tmp/producers_hs_$1.pid"; }
40	wdfile()  { echo "/tmp/producers_hs_$1.wd"; }
41	logfile() { echo "/tmp/producers_hs_$1.log"; }
42	
43	free_port() {
44	  local port="$1" pid
45	  pid=$(cat "$(pidfile "$port")" 2>/dev/null || true)
46	  if [ -n "$pid" ]; then
47	    kill -TERM "-$pid" 2>/dev/null || kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
48	    sleep 1
49	    kill -KILL "-$pid" 2>/dev/null || kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
50	  fi
51	  # Fallback: anything still bound to the port (reaps orphaned maude children).
52	  if command -v fuser >/dev/null 2>&1; then
53	    fuser -k "${port}/tcp" 2>/dev/null || true
54	  fi
55	  local wd; wd=$(cat "$(wdfile "$port")" 2>/dev/null || true)
56	  [ -n "$wd" ] && [ -d "$wd" ] && rm -rf "$wd"
57	  rm -f "$(pidfile "$port")" "$(wdfile "$port")"
58	}

62	  [ -f "$f" ] || { echo "no such file: $f" >&2; return 2; }
63	  [ -x "$HS" ] || { echo "no HS binary at $HS" >&2; return 2; }
64	  free_port "$port"                                # clean any stale bind first
65	  local wd; wd=$(mktemp -d); mkdir -p "$wd/thy"; cp "$f" "$wd/thy/"
66	  echo "$wd" > "$(wdfile "$port")"
67	  # setsid → own process group so `free_port` can signal the whole tree.

70	      --derivcheck-timeout="$DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT" >"$(logfile "$port")" 2>&1 &
71	  echo $! > "$(pidfile "$port")"

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Materialize the distinct `main/*` wildcard tails of every href in the
captured corpus (captured OUTPUT) as R5 byte targets.

Writes workspace/r5_tails/tails.txt: one line per DISTINCT tail (the part after
`/thy/<kind>/<idx>/main/`), prefixed by its corpus occurrence count, sorted.
The R5 corpus test parses each tail and re-renders it, asserting byte identity
(for the families the producer link vocabulary covers) — `method/…` tails are
listed too but marked out-of-vocabulary by the test itself.
"""
import collections
import glob
import json
import os
import re

HERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
CORPUS = os.path.join(HERE, "..", "..", "oracle", "captured_responses")
OUT = os.path.join(HERE, "..", "r5_tails")

URL_ATTR = re.compile(r'(?:href|action|dotsrc)=[\'"]([^\'"]+)[\'"]')
MAIN = re.compile(r"^/thy/(?:trace|equiv)/[^/]+/main/(.+)$")

tails = collections.Counter()
for p in sorted(glob.glob(os.path.join(CORPUS, "*.hs.json"))):
    d = json.load(open(p))
    for url, r in d.get("manifest", {}).items():
        body = r.get("body", "")
        for m in URL_ATTR.finditer(body):
            mm = MAIN.match(m.group(1))
            if mm:
                tails[mm.group(1)] += 1
        if r.get("kind") == "text" and body.startswith("/thy/"):
            mm = MAIN.match(body.strip())
            if mm:
                tails[mm.group(1)] += 1

os.makedirs(OUT, exist_ok=True)
with open(os.path.join(OUT, "tails.txt"), "w") as fh:
    for t in sorted(tails):
        fh.write("%d\t%s\n" % (tails[t], t))
print("wrote %d distinct tails (%d occurrences)" % (len(tails), sum(tails.values())))

Decoding: valid `%XX` → the byte, byte string read as UTF-8 with U+FFFD replacement (`caf%C3%A9`→café, `a%FFb`→`a�b`); INVALID sequences stay literal (`a%zzb`, `a%`, `a%2`, `a%G1b`); `+` is NOT a space [L12]. Heads match exactly and case-sensitively (`MESSAGE`, `cases/RAW` → 404) [L08][L09]. **Acceptance.** Heads: `help` `message` `rules` `tactic` (no args) · `cases/{raw|refined}/{i}/{j}` · `lemma/{name}` · `proof/{lemma}[/seg…]` · `edit/{name}` `add/{pos}` `delete/{name}`. Extra segments after a complete match are IGNORED (`help/extra`, `message/`, `cases/raw/0/0/extra` accepted); missing required args reject (`proof`, `cases/raw/1` → 404); name args accept ANY decoded text including empty (`edit/`, `proof//_` parse); lemma EXISTENCE is resolution, not parse (`proof/nonexistent` → 200 "No such lemma or proof path") [L08][L11]. `sources` is NOT a head; `method/{lemma}/{n}[/…]` is server-accepted but outside the producer link vocabulary (no ThyPath constructor → `parse` returns None; documented interface scope). **Numeric segments** (the two `cases` indices) parse as a Haskell-`reads`-shaped integer prefix [L10]: optional whitespace, balanced parens, optional `-` (space allowed after), one integer lexeme — decimal / `0x` `0o` `0b` any case — then arbitrary junk IGNORED at top level (`1abc`, `1_`, `(1)x` accept) but not inside parens (`(1x)` rejects); a decimal lexeme continuing as a FLOAT rejects (`1.0`, `1e2`); a LEADING underscore rejects (`_1` lexes as an identifier — the underscore-prefix quirk) while interior/trailing ones are junk (`1_0`, `0x_1`). `+1` and `--1` reject.
